source_jobs.title: Senior Mechanical Engineer (Modular Data Center)
source_jobs.description:
Orbital is an AI-first industrial company building hardware from the atoms up. Our goal is to lead an industrial renaissance to advance critical technologies and secure our planet for generations to come.

We’re starting with critical hardware for AI data centers to make them more performant and sustainable. Every Orbital product is invented with our AI platform — uniting AI-automated hardware engineering with AI-designed material science to achieve breakthrough real-world performance.

We have an ambitious mission and need excellent people in all our teams - AI research, operations, advanced materials, mechanical engineering, chemical engineering and manufacturing.

Working at Orbital means working in tightly integrated, vertically integrated teams. We’re looking for people who have a love of physical technology, curiosity in AI and a desire to learn.


ROLE OVERVIEW

Orbital is seeking a highly skilled and innovative Senior Mechanical Engineer to design and optimize advanced Modular Data Centers.

This is a Senior role within the MDC function that combines strategic input and involves hands-on participation in cutting-edge research, including design and layout of mechanical systems for data center equipment and actively participating in and conducting lab experiments to verify the validity and performance of our modular data centers.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

A lead role within Orbital for Modular Data Center design, manufacturing and deployment. As a subject matter expert, you’ll interface with existing and potential clients configuring modular deployments to best suit their needs and suggest improvements.

Design for Modular Data Centers

 - Selection of coils, heat exchangers, chillers, compressors, pumps and other supporting components

 - Working with key stakeholders to recommend designs for performance, manufacturability, and ease of deployment
   
   - Compare the impact on overall efficiency of the data center of different refrigerants for the ambient design condition
   
   - Select compressors, expansion valves, and other supporting systems



Coordination, Review and Approval of Technical Reports and Documentation

 - Review internal engineering work as well as external contractors such as CFD and FEA analysis

 - As the team continues to grow, this role will be responsible for the direction of intermediate engineers, drafters and designers from various disciplines that will be added to the MDC team.

Miscellaneous

 - Participate in the evaluation and selection of potential contract manufacturers

 - Assist in supporting internal and external manufacturing operations including integrating feedback from various stakeholders to continuously improve our modular products

 - Lead role in bringing new and innovative ways to design and manufacture products




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Professional engineering designation with 7+ years of experience in the design, selection, and/or manufacturing of mechanical and electrical systems for data centers, HVAC, and large-scale cooling applications

 - Deep understanding of modular data center architecture — including mechanical/electrical subsystems, applicable codes, data center tiers, and best practices for efficiency and reliability

 - Expertise in chiller and refrigeration system selection, design and optimization

 - Experience with simulation tools (e.g. CFD, FEA) for thermal and fluid dynamics, and hands-on lab experience designing and executing experiments to validate mechanical system performance

 - Ability to interpret experimental data and translate findings into actionable design improvements




WHY JOIN ORBITAL?

 - Competitive salary and equity package - the ability to own part of Orbital as we grow

 - Flexible and generous paid time off

 - Excellent health, dental and vision insurance plan

 - Regular company off-sites to the USA and beyond

 - The experience of working in a cutting-edge organisation dedicated to a better future

Orbital is an equal opp

source_jobs.title: Quality Manager
source_jobs.description:
Orbital is an AI-first industrial company building hardware from the atoms up. Our goal is to lead an industrial renaissance to advance critical technologies and secure our planet for generations to come.

We’re starting with critical hardware for AI data centers to make them more performant and sustainable. Every Orbital product is invented with our AI platform — uniting AI-automated hardware engineering with AI-designed material science to achieve breakthrough real-world performance.

We have an ambitious mission and need excellent people in all our teams - AI research, operations, advanced materials, mechanical engineering, chemical engineering and manufacturing.

Working at Orbital means working in tightly integrated, vertically integrated teams. We’re looking for people who have a love of physical technology, curiosity in AI and a desire to learn.




ROLE OVERVIEW

In this role, you will be building your ideal team of professionals and establishing the quality department for a new suite of data center related products including a 2 Phase Direct to Chip cooling system utilizing a new novel coolant discovered by Orbitals AI technology, Coolant Distribution Units, Dual Use Chiller and Modular Data Centers.

Initially, this role will start with outsourcing production to contract manufacturing requiring the establishment of new processes and procedures for validating and onboarding suppliers.

In our 2nd phase, we will be establishing our in-house manufacturing capabilities requiring building of teams and processes from the ground up.

You will own product and process quality from concept through field life. Build a scalable QMS, prevent defects at the source, and ensure every unit (and module) ships safe, compliant, and reliable.






KEY RESPONSIBILITIES




PHASE 1 — CONTRACT MANUFACTURER VALIDATION, ONBOARDING & QUALITY MANAGEMENT

 - Source & qualify CMs and key suppliers: conduct technical/commercial audits (capacity, process capability, ISO 9001/14001, special processes related to quality, EHS)

 - APQP at CMs: define CTQs/CTPs, control plans, PFMEA/DFMEA alignment, MSA, submission/approval of vendors; oversee FOK pilot builds.

 - Quality agreements & documentation: establish supplier quality agreements, traceability schema (serial/lot genealogy), inspection/test instructions, packaging/labeling/handling standards.

 - Performance management: implement supplier scorecards (OTD, PPM, responsiveness, audit scores); run QBRs; drive SCAR/CAPA with effectiveness verification and aging control.

 - Field & commissioning interface for CM builds: define FAT/SAT witness criteria, release gates, and containment plans; triage escapes with 8D/A3 and feed lessons into CM control plans.
   


PHASE 2 — ESTABLISH THE IN-HOUSE QUALITY ORGANIZATION (NEW MANUFACTURING PLANT)

 - Build the QMS: implement ISO 9001 backbone (coordinate with 14001/45001 where relevant); write SOPs/WIs, MRB/CAPA governance, document control, and e-sign workflows.

 - Factory quality architecture: stand up in-process, and final test (FAT) for CDUs and modular skids; define station-level CTQs, stop-the-line criteria, etc.

 - People & org: hire, train, and coach inspectors, technicians, SQEs/CQEs; set tiered daily management (tier boards, paretos, andons) and a prevention-first culture (poka-yoke, error-proof fixtures).

 - Systems integration: integrate Quality with PLM/ERP/MES for single source of truth; enable digital travelers, barcoding/RFID traceability, and automated data capture from test stands.

 - Ramp & readiness: plan EVT/DVT/PVT at the plant; qualify production lines/process windows; lock control plans and release criteria before rate increase.

 - Compliance & certifications: secure/maintain with the required multi-departmental team members UL/CSA/CE/UKCA, EMC/LVD, UL 508A for panels, pressure equipment (ASME/PED, CRN), refrigerant handling safety, RoHS/REACH documentation.
   


ONGOING, CROSS-PHASE RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Reliability & field qu

source_jobs.title: Machine Learning Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Orbital is an AI-first industrial company building hardware from the atoms up. Our goal is to lead an industrial renaissance to advance critical technologies and secure our planet for generations to come.

We’re starting with critical hardware for AI data centers to make them more performant and sustainable. Every Orbital product is invented with our AI platform — uniting AI-automated hardware engineering with AI-designed material science to achieve breakthrough real-world performance.

We have an ambitious mission and need excellent people in all our teams - AI research, operations, advanced materials, mechanical engineering, chemical engineering and manufacturing.

Working at Orbital means working in tightly integrated, vertically integrated teams. We’re looking for people who have a love of physical technology, curiosity in AI and a desire to learn.



As a Machine Learning Engineer at Orbital, you will architect cutting-edge AI systems for the multi-scale design of physical technologies. When we say multi-scale, we mean it: we build world-class foundation models for simulating both the microscopic motion of atoms and the macroscopic flow of liquids in 1GW data centers. We then co-design across these different scales using the ingenuity of our scientists and engineers, augmented with best-in-class domain agents.

In this role you will set exceptionally high technical standards and drive projects from prototype through to production deployment. First and foremost, we want to work with someone with a love of craftsmanship, continual learning, and building systems that scale. We also value low ego, and a genuine passion for using AI to solve major global industrial technology challenges.



Key Responsibilities

Set the technical bar and ensure engineering excellence

 - Establish and maintain exceptionally high standards for code quality, system architecture and ML research and engineering practices through hands-on coding and technical review

 - Design robust, well-engineered systems that others can build upon, balancing research velocity with production requirements

 - Drive technical decisions on model selection, training approaches and deployment strategies

Deliver high-impact AI projects across diverse domains

 - Develop and deploy AI solutions across the entire technology development pipeline- computational chemistry simulations, agentic workflows and beyond

 - Rapidly upskill in new technical areas through close collaboration with domain experts (no prior chemistry or materials experience required)

 - Demonstrate strong implementation skills through hands-on development, contributing significantly to the codebase

 - Balance research rigour with pragmatic engineering to deliver production-ready systems at scale

Push the frontier of ML research

 - Design and implement novel ML architectures for complex scientific domains, with work that meets publication standards at top-tier conferences

 - Drive research projects from conception through to deployment, showing initiative and technical depth

 - Engage continuously with the latest ML literature, staying current with developments in foundation models, generative AI and scientific machine learning



What We're Looking For

 - Significant software engineering and ML experience, with depth in training, evaluating and deploying AI models - demonstrated through industry work

 - Proven experience training, evaluating and productionising AI models at scale, with deep understanding of the full ML lifecycle from research to deployment

 - Strong engineering fundamentals with the ability to write high-quality, maintainable code and architect robust systems

 - A strong ability to reason about algorithms, system design, linear algebra, probabilistic concepts and ML engineering trade-offs

 - An ability to debug complex machine learning systems through meticulous attention to detail, testing of edge cases and carefully selected ablations

 - A genuine interest in building

source_jobs.title: Electrical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Orbital is an AI-first industrial company building hardware from the atoms up. Our goal is to lead an industrial renaissance to advance critical technologies and secure our planet for generations to come.

We’re starting with critical hardware for AI data centers to make them more performant and sustainable. Every Orbital product is invented with our AI platform — uniting AI-automated hardware engineering with AI-designed material science to achieve breakthrough real-world performance.

We have an ambitious mission and need excellent people in all our teams - AI research, operations, advanced materials, mechanical engineering, chemical engineering and manufacturing.

Working at Orbital means working in tightly integrated, vertically integrated teams. We’re looking for people who have a love of physical technology, curiosity in AI and a desire to learn.




ROLE OVERVIEW

The Electrical Engineer will play a critical leadership role in the design, development, and delivery of advanced electrical and controls systems for our engineered products and modular data centers. This position is responsible for owning the full electrical design lifecycle - from early-stage concept development to detailed construction-ready electrical installation drawing packages - using Autodesk Inventor, AutoCAD Electrical, and associated digital tools.

The successful candidate will lead electrical system architecture, coordinate with mechanical engineering, software teams, external stakeholders, future production personnel and ensure all electrical and controls designs meet safety, applicable codes, performance, regulatory and manufacturability requirements.






KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


ELECTRICAL DESIGN AND ENGINEERING

 - Develop complete electrical system architectures for industrial equipment, automation systems, and modular deployments including cable sizing and % fill calculations for tray and conduit.

 - Produce detailed electrical installation drawings, wiring diagrams, schematics, harness/conduit routes, panel layouts, PLC I/O maps, and sensor/actuator integration.

 - Model electrical components and cable paths within Autodesk Inventor assemblies, including point-to-point routing, conduit/cable tray design, and interference checks.

 - Build and maintain electrical content libraries, templates, and standards to streamline repeatable design patterns.
   


CONTROLS ENGINEERING

 - Define control strategies, functional specifications, and instrumentation selection for automated systems.

 - Collaborate with client teams on PLC programming requirements, HMI architecture, and network/communication topology.

 - Ensure controls logic, safety circuits, and emergency-stop logic comply with design standards (e.g., NFPA 79, IEC/UL, CSA).
   


DOCUMENTATION & DELIVERABLES

 - Generate release-ready drawing packages including electrical installation drawings, panel fabrication drawings, BOMs, cable schedules, single-line diagrams, and load calculations.

 - Lead internal design reviews and cross-disciplinary coordination to ensure seamless integration of electrical design with mechanical, thermal, and structural systems.

 - Maintain engineering change documentation, revision control, and compliance with company quality procedures.
   


TECHNICAL LEADERSHIP

 - Mentor current mechanical (and future electrical) engineers, designers, and drafters on best practices for electrical and controls installation to be able to assist in construction drawing creation.

 - Champion design automation workflows with the AI team, including parameterized assemblies, iLogic, and Inventor-based electrical design acceleration.

 - Engage in vendor coordination and review of supplier component specifications (switchgear, RPPs, PLCs, VFDs, sensors, etc.).
   


COMPLIANCE & STANDARDS

 - Ensure all designs meet relevant electrical codes and standards (NEC, CEC, UL/CSA, NFPA 70/79, IEC).

 - Conduct arc-flash calculations, load studies, and grounding/bonding design where

source_jobs.title: Content Manager
source_jobs.description:
Orbital is an AI-first industrial company building hardware from the atoms up. Our goal is to lead an industrial renaissance to advance critical technologies and secure our planet for generations to come.

We’re starting with critical hardware for AI data centers to make them more performant and sustainable. Every Orbital product is invented with our AI platform — uniting AI-automated hardware engineering with AI-designed material science to achieve breakthrough real-world performance.

We have an ambitious mission and need excellent people in all our teams - AI research, operations, advanced materials, mechanical engineering, chemical engineering and manufacturing.

Working at Orbital means working in tightly integrated, vertically integrated teams. We’re looking for people who have a love of physical technology, curiosity in AI and a desire to learn.



We are looking for a Content Manager who knows how to turn complex ideas into commercially effective writing and campaigns. You'll own Orbital's content strategy from the ground up: defining the editorial vision, creating high-impact content across formats, and ensuring everything ties back to pipeline and growth. You'll work closely with GTM, Sales, Product, and Science teams to produce content that resonates with technical audiences, and you'll use data to continuously sharpen what's working.



Deep expertise in materials science isn't a prerequisite; intellectual curiosity and the ability to get up to speed quickly on complex topics is. If you're energised by the challenge of making hard things clear, and by the opportunity to build something that genuinely matters, this role is for you.






KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Content Strategy & Commercial Impact

 - Own and drive Orbital's content strategy with a clear line of sight to commercial outcomes - pipeline, lead conversion, and revenue acceleration - balancing long-term brand building with short-term GTM needs.

 - Act as a strategic partner to Sales, identifying gaps in the buyer's journey and building the assets that move prospects forward, from awareness content to technical deal-closers.

 - Use data and performance insights to measure effectiveness, iterate quickly, and make the case for where to invest next.

Editorial Ownership & Delivery

 - Own Orbital's editorial voice, tone, and standards - establishing the processes (briefing, drafting, review, publication) and acting as an editorial partner who enables subject matter experts to contribute without sacrificing quality.

 - Produce thought leadership connecting Orbital's materials innovation to pressing challenges in data centres, energy efficiency, cooling, and sustainability.

 - Lead production of high-impact content across formats: presentations, product specs, technical blog posts, and scientific explainers - using AI tools to work efficiently without sacrificing rigour.

 - Work cross-functionally with Sales, Product, Science, and Leadership to originate ideas and ensure content is accurate, relevant, and aligned to business priorities.

 - Track developments across AI and data centre infrastructure to keep content timely and credible.

Community & Distribution

 - Build Orbital's presence across key communities - data centre, infrastructure, AI research, chemistry, and engineering.

 - Develop content that strengthens positioning with technically fluent decision-makers globally.

 - Pioneer new ways to activate content and grow Orbital's reach.






WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Proven ability to independently devise and execute a content strategy that drives a GTM function. You understand how to use content as a lever for revenue.

 - Experience working closely with Sales and GTM to identify content gaps in the buyer's journey and address them with high-impact assets.

 - Outstanding writing and editing skills, with strong long-form examples; proficient in using LLMs and tools to execute efficiently and at scale.

 - Experience in or an affinity for deep te

source_jobs.title: Modular Data Center Sales Executive
source_jobs.description:
Orbital is an AI-first industrial company building hardware from the atoms up. Our goal is to lead an industrial renaissance to advance critical technologies and secure our planet for generations to come.

We’re starting with critical hardware for AI data centers to make them more performant and sustainable. Every Orbital product is invented with our AI platform — uniting AI-automated hardware engineering with AI-designed material science to achieve breakthrough real-world performance.

We have an ambitious mission and need excellent people in all our teams - AI research, operations, advanced materials, mechanical engineering, chemical engineering and manufacturing.

Working at Orbital means working in tightly integrated, vertically integrated teams. We’re looking for people who have a love of physical technology, curiosity in AI and a desire to learn.




ROLE OVERVIEW



Orbital are searching for a Sales Executive to lead business development for Orbital's modular data center platform. This role is built for professionals with an EPMC (Engineering, Procurement, Management & Construction) sales background who understand that selling infrastructure means selling trust - trust in engineering rigor, delivery timelines and long-term performance.

You will own the full sales cycle: identifying operators under capacity pressure, shaping solutions alongside our engineering team, navigating complex procurement processes and closing multi-million-dollar deployments. You won't be handing off a spec sheet. You'll be in the room with our engineers, our manufacturing leads and our delivery teams - because at Orbital, sales is a technical discipline.

 
 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Pipeline & Market Development:

 - Identify and pursue opportunities with hyperscalers, colocation providers, enterprise IT, and telecom operators — tracking competitive dynamics, emerging deployment models and regional demand signals to inform go-to-market strategy.
   

Consultative Selling

 - Translate client requirements across power, cooling, redundancy, and deployment timelines into tailored modular configurations, scoping complex engagements with the rigour of EPMC but the pace of a product business.
   

Stakeholder & Bid Management

 - Lead multi-stakeholder sales cycles from first engagement through to signed contract — owning RFP/RFQ responses, competitive pricing, and negotiation of MSAs, payment milestones, and performance guarantees.

Account Growth

 - Build strategic account plans oriented towards repeat and expansion business; the goal is multi-deployment partnerships, not one-time transactions.

Cross-Functional Coordination

 - Act as the internal voice of the customer — aligning sales commitments with engineering feasibility, manufacturing capacity and delivery schedules, with direct access to the people doing the work.

 


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - A proven technical sales professional with 7+ years closing complex capital equipment or turnkey infrastructure deals - in EPMC, mission-critical, or heavy MEP environments, with a track record in the $5M - $100M+ range

 - Deep fluency in design-build and EPC contract structures, including change order management, milestone-based delivery, and the financial rigour that comes with capital-intensive sales (TCO, ROI, business case development)

 - Solid grounding in data centre fundamentals — power distribution, UPS/generator systems, precision cooling (DX, chilled water, liquid-to-chip), and Tier classification — with experience selling to owner-operators, developers, or general contractors

 - Ideally, direct experience with modular, prefabricated, or containerised infrastructure solutions, and existing relationships with hyperscalers, colo operators, or data centre developers

 - Familiarity with high-density AI/HPC deployment requirements and liquid cooling architectures — or the technical curiosity and infrastructure background to get there quickly

 - Comfortable with 40–60% domestic travel an

source_jobs.title: Thermal Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Orbital is an AI-first industrial company building hardware from the atoms up. Our goal is to lead an industrial renaissance to advance critical technologies and secure our planet for generations to come.

We’re starting with critical hardware for AI data centers to make them more performant and sustainable. Every Orbital product is invented with our AI platform — uniting AI-automated hardware engineering with AI-designed material science to achieve breakthrough real-world performance.

We have an ambitious mission and need excellent people in all our teams - AI research, operations, advanced materials, mechanical engineering, chemical engineering and manufacturing.

Working at Orbital means working in tightly integrated, vertically integrated teams. We’re looking for people who have a love of physical technology, curiosity in AI and a desire to learn.


ROLE OVERVIEW

Orbital is seeking a highly skilled and innovative Thermal Engineer to develop next-generation cooling technologies for high-density data center applications. The role spans the full development cycle: from modeling and simulations to cold plate design, condenser optimization, physical prototyping, flow loop construction, and system-level testing. You will work directly with novel refrigerators and proprietary AI toolsets to push the boundaries of speed and efficiency in data center cooling.

 
 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

This is a key role within Orbital’s cooling technology portfolio from design to deployment. As a subject matter expert, you’ll need equal comfort at the whiteboard and the bench. You will design, build and operate two-phase test loops, analyse performance data and rapidly iterate towards solutions that can scale from prototype to product.

Physical Prototyping and Testing

 - Evaluate candidate refrigerants based on thermodynamic properties, saturation temperature, safety classification, material compatibility, and regulatory requirements for integration into two-phase cooling designs.

 - Design and optimize evaporator cold plates including channel geometry selection, inlet restriction sizing and exit vapor quality targeting

 - Evaluate and develop enhanced boiling surfaces to improve nucleation site density and increase critical heat flux margins

 - Construct thermal test vehicles to evaluate cold plate performance under control and representative heat flux conditions

 - Operate two-phase test loops to characterize flow, generate boiling curves and CHF data.

 - Translate experimental findings into design rules and validated simulation inputs

 

Modeling and SImulations

 - Perform system-level thermodynamic analyses: pressure-enthalpy cycle mapping, charge optimization, subcooling control, and component sizing for two-phase flow

 - Use or direct CFD or other modeling tools to evaluate cold plate designs, flow distribution in manifolds and thermal stresses in assemblies.

 - Build or contribute to system-level models for cycle analysis, parametric studies and impact of fluid properties on total performance.

Miscellaneous

 - Support Obrital’s proprietary fluid development efforts by contributing to compatibility testing, accelerated aging studies, and performance characterization.

 - Specify materials, bonding processes, and manufacturing methods for prototype and production cold plate units.

 - As the team continues to grow, this role will be responsible for the direction of intermediate engineers, designers, and researchers contributing to two-phase development

 - Produce technical reports, test plans, and design specifications for internal and external stakeholders.

 
 


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Degree in mechanical engineering, chemical engineering or a closely related discipline with 5+ years of experience.

 - Demonstrated experience in single or two-phase heat transfer, boiling phenomena or refrigeration system design

 - Proficiency in conducting thermodynamic analysis to optimize cooling system performance, energy efficien

notes: AI-first industrial company with strong engineering focus
